title: Agrostemma thessalum
taxonomy: Agrostemma thessalum is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It belongs to the genus Agrostemma, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as corn-cocksfoot or corn poppy. Within the Caryophyllaceae family, Agrostemma thessalum is classified as a member of the subfamily Caryophylloideae. The plant is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found growing in dry, sandy areas. Further classification of Agrostemma thessalum includes its order Caryophyllales and division Magnoliophyta.

intro: Agrostemma thessalum, commonly known as red poppy or corn poppy, is a flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Agrostemma thessalum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make tinctures and teas, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
